Improve get_commits_for_day() function in weekly.py

This commit improves the get_commits_for_day() function in the weekly.py module. The changes include:

- Added optional `since` and `until` parameters to the function to allow specifying exact timestamp boundaries for the commit range, rather than using midnight-to-midnight.
- Updated the `--since` and `--until` flags passed to `git log` to use the ISO 8601 timestamp format (`%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S`) instead of just the date.
- Adjusted the logic in the weekly_workflow() function to use the new `since` and `until` parameters when processing the first and last days of the date range.

These changes will improve the accuracy of the commit retrieval for the weekly summary, especially on the start and end dates of the range.

Pass exact boundary timestamps to get_commits_for_day

Previously, get_commits_for_day always used midnight-to-midnight date
strings, causing commits at the start or end of the requested range to
be missed when the caller supplied sub-day timestamps. The function now
accepts optional since and until datetime parameters that override the
default boundaries, and weekly_workflow passes start_date and end_date
as those overrides on the first and last days of the iteration.

Add targeted property-identifying search for personal property references

Enhanced the `run_ask` function to detect personal property references (e.g., "my address", "my lot") in the question and perform a targeted search for property-identifying information. Added a new regex pattern `_PERSONAL_PATTERNS` to identify such references, executed a dedicated search for legal descriptions, lot numbers, and property addresses, and reserved up to 3 slots in the final results for these high-priority chunks. Modified the result deduplication and selection logic to incorporate these identity results while maintaining the original top-k constraint.

Modified:
- backend/app/services.py — Added regex pattern `_PERSONAL_PATTERNS` for detecting personal property references, implemented targeted identity search with `run_search` for property-identifying terms, reserved up to 3 slots for identity results in `reserved_identity`, adjusted `effective_top_k` calculation, refined result deduplication and merging logic, and updated context collection to include document headers for richer property context

Enhance search logic to prioritize personal property-related results

Add targeted search for personal property references in question handling

Expand `run_ask()` to detect property-related queries and prioritize legal/identifying chunks. Introduce `_PERSONAL_PATTERNS` regex for matching terms like "my address" or "my lot". Add identity search logic with `SearchRequest` for terms like "legal description" and "lot number", reserving up to 3 slots for these results. Modify top-K selection to account for reserved identity results, prepending them to final output while deduplicating by chunk ID. Update context retrieval to include document headers for richer property context.

Modified:
- backend/app/services.py —
  - Added `_PERSONAL_PATTERNS` regex to detect property-related queries
  - Added identity search logic for "legal description", "lot number", and similar terms
  - Introduced `reserved_identity` list to prioritize up to 3 property-identifying chunks
  - Modified top-K selection to use `effective_top_k` accounting for reserved slots
  - Added chunk deduplication for identity results via `existing_chunks` set
  - Updated context retrieval to include document headers (chunk 0) when available
  - Changed scoring logic to sort chunks by score before selection
  - Added prepending of identity results to final output while maintaining deduplication

Enhance run_ask() to prioritize property-identifying search results

The run_ask() function now performs an additional targeted search when the user's question references personal property, and prioritizes results that identify the property. The function also includes the document header chunk in the context for top results.

Improve search result deduplication and context in run_ask()

After reviewing the diff, the key changes are:

1. Added a new search step to find property-identifying information (legal descriptions, lot numbers, etc.) when the question references personal property.
2. Improved the deduplication logic to first take the top result from each document, then fill remaining slots with the next-best chunks from those documents.
3. Reserved up to 3 slots for the property-identifying search results to ensure they are included.
4. Included the first chunk (document header) of each document in the final context, in addition to the matched chunk and its neighbors.

This commit addresses these improvements to the search result deduplication and context retrieval in the run_ask() function.

Add Tasks tab with guided generation, refinement pipeline, and gambit benchmarks

This commit implements the complete Tasks workflow for guided document generation with real-time prompt quality scoring, multi-phase document discovery, form-aware output formatting, and comprehensive test coverage.

Modified:

- README.md — Added Tasks section documenting workflow (prompt → find documents → review → generate → refine → export), document discovery pipeline (hybrid search, entity extraction, abbreviation search, prompt decomposition, Cohere Rerank, score cutoff, auto-include forms), form detection behavior, task model selection (NVIDIA Nemotron Super), task history persistence (localStorage, configurable max), and ZIP export package structure with writeup + source citations

- backend/app/main.py — Added /search/refine endpoint for prompt decomposition + reranking; updated task_generate() to detect form/application documents and switch system prompt to plain-paragraph mode; implemented chunk-level retrieval via search_chunks_grouped() for precision context building; added form-aware post-processing with Nova Micro paragraph splitting; changed default BEDROCK_TASK_SINGLE_MODEL_ID from amazon.nova-pro-v1:0 to nvidia.nemotron-super-3-120b; updated generate_export_package() to build structured archive with numbered source directories, relevance.txt per document, and prompt + citations in writeup.txt; changed gap-to-email system prompt from homeowner-specific to generic vendor/contractor language

- backend/app/schemas.py — Added expanded_query field to AskResponse for query expansion transparency

- backend/app/search.py — Added document_ids and top_k parameters to search_chunks() for filtering and grouped retrieval; implemented search_chunks_grouped() to return dict[doc_id: text] for chunk-level context assembly; added decompose_prompt() using Nova Micro to extract structured intent (action, target_document, vendor, product, subject, output_type); implemented refine_document_selection() with four-phase pipeline: prompt decomposition, entity/vendor matching, Cohere Rerank v3.5 with content-focused query, and score cutoff (8% of top score) with entity match preservation

This commit adds new test files for the Form Fill Gambit, Retrieval + Generation E2E Gambit, and Package Archive Structure tests. It also modifies several existing files to implement the changes required by these new tests.

The key changes are:

1. Added new test files in the `tests/` directory:
   - `gambit_form_fill.py`: Runs a multi-model, multi-document-set, multi-prompt test to find the best combination for generating an accurate "Description of Proposed Modification" form field.
   - `gambit_retrieval_e2e.py`: Tests the full Tasks tab pipeline, from prompt to generated content, measuring retrieval recall/precision and end-to-end quality.
   - `gambit_package_archive.py`: Generates a structured ZIP archive with the writeup and source documents, organized by relevance.

2. Modified the backend to support the new tests:
   - Added a `/search/refine` endpoint in `backend/app/main.py` to implement prompt decomposition, entity matching, and Cohere Rerank for improved document selection.
   - Updated the `tasks/generate` endpoint to handle form-specific formatting and auto-include form documents when the prompt implies a form.
   - Expanded the `ask` endpoint to support query expansion and dual-search for better context building.

3. Updated the frontend in `frontend/src/main.ts` to integrate the new Tasks tab features:
   - Prompt quality meter with real-time search-based scoring.
   - Task history persistence and management.
   - Workflow logging for transparency.
   - Export options for Markdown, DOCX, and structured ZIP packages.

4. Expanded the `TEST_STRATEGY.md` file with details on the new test suites, their purpose, and the key findings/recommendations that were applied to the application.

The commit ensures that the application's core functionality for form field generation, retrieval-based content production, and structured output packaging is thoroughly tested and validated.

- Updated the `README.md` file to include instructions for running the gambit tests.
- Modified the `frontend/src/main.ts` file to include a new task history feature and to display the task log.
- Added new files to the `tests/results` directory for storing the results of the gambit tests.

Implement guided document generation workflow with Tasks tab

This commit introduces a guided document generation workflow via the new Tasks tab, enabling users to generate structured content grounded in indexed documents. The implementation includes multi-phase document discovery, form detection, task history persistence, and structured export capabilities.

Key features:
- Real-time prompt quality scoring using embedding search
- Multi-phase document discovery: hybrid search, entity extraction, abbreviation search, prompt decomposition, and Cohere Rerank
- Form-aware generation that switches to plain-paragraph output when application/form documents are detected
- Task history persistence with configurable retention
- Structured ZIP export containing writeup, source documents, and relevance summaries
- New endpoints for document refinement and export packaging

Add Tasks workflow with guided document discovery and form-aware generation

Implemented a complete guided document generation workflow in the Tasks tab, featuring real-time prompt quality scoring, multi-phase document discovery, automatic form detection, and structured export packaging. The system uses chunk-level retrieval for precision, prompt decomposition for better search, and Cohere Rerank to filter noise. Validated via comprehensive gambit testing across 10 models, 4 document sets, and 3 prompt complexity levels (120 combinations total). Default task model set to NVIDIA Nemotron Super based on benchmark results (89.3/100 avg score, 100/100 on detailed prompts).

Add Tasks tab with form detection, refinement pipeline, and gambit benchmarks

This commit delivers the full Tasks tab workflow: guided document discovery via hybrid search + entity extraction + Cohere Rerank + prompt decomposition, chunk-level context retrieval for precision, automatic form/application detection that switches generation to plain-paragraph mode with a form-aware system prompt, and a post-processing step using Nova Micro to split wall-of-text output at topic transitions. The default task model is changed from Nova Pro to NVIDIA Nemotron Super (100/100 gambit score, 2.7s) based on benchmark results. The export package endpoint is restructured into a numbered sources/ directory with per-document relevance.txt files. Three gambit test scripts and their full result sets (120 form-fill combinations across 7 models, 4 doc sets, 3 prompt levels, plus retrieval e2e runs) are added alongside updated documentation.

Modified:
- README.md — added Tasks tab section, Document Discovery Pipeline, Form Detection, Task History, Export Package structure, new API endpoints /search/refine and /generate/export-package
- deployment/docker/README.md — added Tasks guided generation workflow steps
- test/TEST_STRATEGY.md — added sections 5 (Form Fill Gambit), 6 (Retrieval E2E Gambit), 7 (Refinement Pipeline Gambit) with scoring rubrics and findings
- backend/app/main.py — added POST /search/refine endpoint calling refine_document_selection(); added form/application detection in task_generate() that sets is_form_task and form_doc_titles; switched context building to search_chunks_grouped() when skip_auto_search is set; added form-aware system_prompt override; added REMINDER suffix to user_msg for form tasks; added Nova Micro post-processing to split wall-of-text paragraphs; changed default BEDROCK_TASK_SINGLE_MODEL_ID from nova-pro to nvidia.nemotron-super-3-120b; restructured generate_export_package() to produce numbered sources/ dirs with relevance.txt per document and writeup.txt with prompt + citations; generalized gap-to-email prompt and system text removing homeowner-specific hardcoded strings; updated admin_get_config() default for task_single
- backend/app/schemas.py — added expanded_query field (default "") to AskResponse
- backend/app/search.py — added document_ids and top_k parameters to search_chunks() with filter_clauses injection; added search_chunks_grouped() function for chunk-level grouped retrieval by doc_id; added decompose_prompt() using Nova Micro to extract action/vendor/product/subject/target_document; added refine_document_selection() implementing 4-phase pipeline (decompose, entity tag, Cohere Rerank v3.5, score cutoff at 8% of top)
- backend/app/services.py — generalized _SYSTEM_PROMPT removing house-document-specific language; added query expansion step in run_ask() using Nova Micro; added dual search (expanded + original) with chunk-level dedup merge; added expanded_query to AskResponse return
- frontend/src/main.ts — added askLog, gapLog, taskLog, taskPromptScore, taskPromptLabel, taskPromptChecking, taskSavedHistory, taskHistoryMax, taskShowHistory to reactive state; added loadTaskHistory(), saveTaskToHistory(), loadTaskFromHistory(), deleteTaskHistoryEntry(), clearAllTaskHistory(), setTaskHistoryMax() functions with localStorage persistence; added scorePrompt() with 500ms debounce calling /search for live prompt quality meter (5-bar colored indicator); added renderLog() helper rendering collapsible workflow log widget; wired taskLog entries throughout runTask() and Find Documents click handler; added /search/refine call in Find Documents with score >= 25 filter on entity searches; added auto-include form document logic when hasFormIntent and no form doc found; added task history panel UI with per-entry delete and Clear All; added prompt quality meter bars in step 1; changed export-package call to pass prompt and use task_package prefix; added Task History max selector in Settings; added askLog and gapLog instrumentation in submit() and gap-to-email handler; changed document review label colors from dark to light theme

Add Tasks tab with guided document generation workflow

This commit introduces a new "Tasks" tab that guides users through a structured document generation workflow. The workflow includes prompt quality scoring, document discovery with hybrid search and entity extraction, chunk-level retrieval for precision, form-aware generation, and export packaging.

The implementation adds:
- A new `/search/refine` endpoint that decomposes prompts and reranks candidates using Cohere Rerank
- A new `/generate/export-package` endpoint that produces a structured ZIP archive with writeup and source documents
- Form detection logic that switches to plain-paragraph output when forms are in the selected document set
- Chunk-level retrieval via `search_chunks_grouped()` to reduce context size and improve precision
- Task history persistence in localStorage with configurable retention
- A workflow log widget that shows each step's status and timing

Enhance banking statement processing with account metadata and transaction extraction

This commit adds comprehensive account-level metadata capture for banking transactions, implements advanced OCR-based transaction extraction from statement text, and improves system health monitoring. Banking statements now preserve account type, holder name, account number, and last-four digits. The document service enriches payloads with account information extracted from statement headers and transaction rows, while fallback text parsing recovers transactions when structured data is incomplete. Database schema and transaction insertion now support these new fields. A health check endpoint monitors Django, Redis, and Celery component status. Pricing configuration now resolves paths dynamically and caches the source location.

Add document classification and upload history UI with Plaid roadmap

This commit introduces a document classification system to automatically categorize uploaded PDFs, adds a new upload history UI component to track document processing, and includes a Plaid integration roadmap. The changes enhance document management capabilities and prepare for future Plaid API integration. 58 new cache files (29 Bedrock JSON and 29 Textract TXT) for paycheck documents spanning January 2025 through February 2026 are added.

New:
- PLAID_ROADMAP.md — roadmap document outlining Plaid API integration strategy
- app/services/document_classifier.py — service implementing DocumentClassifier with classify_document() method using keyword-based classification
- app/ui/upload_history.py — UI component displaying upload history table with document metadata and processing status
- config/document_classification.yaml — configuration file defining document type patterns and classification rules
- .cache/paycheck/bedrock/*.json (29 files) — cached Bedrock analysis results for paycheck PDFs from 2025-01-07 through 2026-02-20
- .cache/paycheck/textract/*.txt (29 files) — cached Textract OCR output for corresponding paycheck PDFs

Modified:
- .gitignore — added .cache/ directory exclusion pattern
- app/integrations/aws_bedrock.py — added caching logic with cache_key parameter and file I/O for JSON cache
- app/integrations/aws_textract.py — added caching logic with cache_key parameter and file I/O for text cache
- app/services/banking_service.py — integrated DocumentClassifier for automatic document type detection
- app/services/document_service.py — added classify_document() method calling DocumentClassifier
- app/services/paycheck_service.py — integrated DocumentClassifier and added cache support
- app/services/utility_ingestion_service.py — integrated DocumentClassifier for utility document classification
- app/ui/layout.py — added upload_history import and integrated upload history component into sidebar
- documents/tasks.py — added document classification step in process_document task
- pages/2_Banking_Summary.py — integrated upload history display
- pages/3_Utility_Summary.py — integrated upload history display
- pages/4_Paycheck_Summary.py — integrated upload history display
- pages/6_Documents.py — added document classification column and filter
- pages/9_Settings.py — added document classification configuration section
- requirements.txt — added pyyaml dependency for YAML configuration parsing

Add document classifier, upload history UI, and paycheck cache

This commit introduces a DocumentClassifier service backed by a new YAML configuration, a reusable upload_history Streamlit component, and a PLAID_ROADMAP planning document. AWS Bedrock and Textract integrations are updated alongside banking, paycheck, utility, and document services. All summary and settings pages are revised to consume the new classifier and history UI, and 29 paycheck PDFs spanning January 2025 through February 2026 are added to the local Bedrock and Textract caches.

This commit updates the README file to document the new model selection workflow introduced in the Bedrock CLI. It adds a new section explaining how users can view available model families, select a specific model, and persist the selection for use in subsequent Bedrock operations.

Modified:
- README.md — Added new 'Model selection' section describing how to choose a Bedrock model

Pass exact boundary timestamps to get_commits_for_day

Previously, get_commits_for_day always used midnight-to-midnight date
strings, causing commits at the start or end of the requested range to
be missed when the caller supplied sub-day timestamps. The function now
accepts optional `since` and `until` datetime parameters that override
the default boundaries, and weekly_workflow passes `start_date` and
`end_date` as those overrides on the first and last days respectively.
Timestamp formatting was also changed from date-only to ISO-8601
datetime strings (%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S).

Modified:
- src/gitops_summary/weekly.py — added `since` and `until` optional parameters to `get_commits_for_day`, switched git log `--since`/`--until` format to full datetime strings, and updated `weekly_workflow` to pass exact boundary timestamps on edge days

Improve search result deduplication and context in AskResponse

The commit improves the deduplication and context-building logic in the `run_ask()` function of the `backend/app/services.py` file:

- Adds a new step to perform a targeted search for property-identifying information (legal descriptions, lot numbers, etc.) if the question references personal property.
- Reserves up to 3 slots in the final top-k results for the highest-scoring property-identifying chunks.
- Improves the deduplication logic to first take the highest-scoring chunk from each document, then fill remaining slots with the next-best chunks from already-selected documents.
- Includes the first chunk (document header) of each selected document in the final context, as it often contains key identifying information.
- Ensures that the final context includes the matched chunk, the previous chunk (if any), and the next chunk (if any) for richer context around each top result.

Add personal-property query detection and identity-chunk injection in run_ask

When a question contains personal-property references ("my address", "my lot", etc.), run_ask now performs a secondary hybrid search for property-identifying terms (legal description, lot number, parcel, assessor) and reserves up to three of those results as priority context. The smart-dedup pass was also tightened: chunks are now explicitly sorted by score before selection, the fill pass collects and sorts remaining candidates before appending, and reserved identity results are prepended to the final top list. Context assembly was extended to prepend chunk 0 (the document header) for each document when it differs from the matched chunk, surfacing key identifying information like property address and legal description.

This commit adds new test files for the Form Fill Gambit, Retrieval + Generation E2E Gambit, and Package Archive Structure tests. It also modifies several existing files to implement the changes required by these new tests.

The key changes are:

1. Added new test files in the `tests/` directory:
   - `gambit_form_fill.py`: Tests the form field generation across multiple models, document sets, and prompt complexities.
   - `gambit_package_archive.py`: Tests the structure and contents of the generated package archive.
   - `gambit_retrieval_e2e.py`: Tests the end-to-end retrieval and generation pipeline using only a typed prompt.

2. Added new result files in the `tests/results/form_fill_gambit/` directory, containing the output of the Form Fill Gambit test runs.

3. Added a new `IMPROVEMENT_JOURNEY.md` file in the `tests/results/retrieval_e2e_gambit/` directory, documenting the changes made to improve the Retrieval + Generation E2E Gambit.

4. Updated the `README.md` file to document the new Tasks tab functionality, including the guided generation workflow, document discovery pipeline, form detection, and task history management.

5. Modified several backend files to implement the new `/search/refine` endpoint, which uses prompt decomposition and Cohere Rerank to improve document selection quality.

6. Updated the frontend `main.ts` file to integrate the new Tasks tab features, including the prompt quality meter, task history management, and workflow logging.

7. Expanded the `TEST_STRATEGY.md` file with details on the new gambit tests and their findings.

The goal of these changes is to improve the overall quality and robustness of the document generation and retrieval capabilities of the application, as validated by the new gambit tests.

Add Tasks tab with guided generation, retrieval refinement, and form-aware output

This release adds a complete guided document generation workflow (Tasks tab) with multi-phase retrieval refinement, form detection, task history persistence, and structured ZIP export. The backend now supports chunk-level retrieval for precision, prompt decomposition + Cohere Rerank for noise reduction, and form-aware system prompts that auto-detect application documents and switch to plain-paragraph output. The frontend includes a real-time prompt quality meter, workflow logs, task history with localStorage persistence, and comprehensive gambit test suites validating model performance across document sets and prompt complexity levels.

Modified:

- README.md — Added Tasks section with workflow steps, document discovery pipeline, form detection, task model selection, task history, and ZIP export structure; updated model list to include Task Model (NVIDIA Nemotron Super); added new API endpoints (/search/refine, /generate/export-package)
- backend/app/main.py — Added /search/refine endpoint for document refinement via prompt decomposition + Cohere Rerank; enhanced /tasks/generate with form detection (checks document_type and title for "application"/"form"), chunk-level retrieval via search_chunks_grouped() for precision, form-aware system prompt override, and post-processing to split wall-of-text into paragraphs using Nova Micro; restructured /generate/export-package to create timestamped archive with sources/ subdirectory, relevance.txt per document, and prompt+citations in writeup.txt; changed default BEDROCK_TASK_SINGLE_MODEL_ID from amazon.nova-pro-v1:0 to nvidia.nemotron-super-3-120b; updated gap-to-email system prompt to remove homeowner-specific language ("someone" instead of "homeowner", removed hardcoded signature)
- backend/app/schemas.py — Added expanded_query field to AskResponse to expose query expansion results
- backend/app/search.py — Added document_ids and top_k parameters to search_chunks() for filtering and grouped retrieval; added search_chunks_grouped() function to return dict[doc_id: text] for chunk-level context building; added decompose_prompt() function using Nova Micro to extract structured intent (action, target_document, vendor, product, subject); added refine_document_selection() function implementing 4-phase refinement: prompt decomposition, entity matching, Cohere Rerank with content-focused query, and score cutoff (8% of top score)
- backend/app/services.py — Enhanced run_ask() with query expansion step using Nova Micro to rewrite questions with technical terms; dual search (expanded + original) with deduplication; added expanded_query to AskResponse
- deployment/docker/README.md — Added Tasks section describing the guided generation workflow (prompt → Find Documents → review → Generate → Refine → Export)
- frontend/src/main.ts — Added task workflow state (taskPromptScore, taskPromptLabel, taskPromptChecking, taskLog, taskSavedHistory, taskHistoryMax, taskShowHistory); added scorePrompt() function with debounced embedding search to rate prompt specificity (1-5 scale); added loadTaskHistory(), saveTaskToHistory(), loadTaskFromHistory(), deleteTaskHistoryEntry(), clearAllTaskHistory(), setTaskHistoryMax() for localStorage persistence; added renderLog() helper to display workflow logs in collapsible details; enhanced task search to include entity extraction, initials search, refinement via /search/refine endpoint, and auto-include form documents when prompt implies form but none found; added prompt quality meter UI with 5-bar visualization; added task history panel with timestamp and delete buttons; added workflow logs to Ask, Task, and Gap-to-Email tabs; updated export-package call to include prompt parameter and use task_package filename prefix
- test/TEST_STRATEGY.md — Added sections 5-7: Form Fill Gambit (120 model × doc set × prompt combinations, 100-point scoring), Retrieval + Generation E2E Gambit (5 prompt variations testing full pipeline), and Refinement Pipeline Gambit (prompt decomposition + Cohere Rerank validation)

New:

- tests/gambit_form_fill.py — Multi-axis test harness: 10 models × 4 document sets × 3 prompt levels = 120 combinations; judges each output on factual accuracy, hallucination rate, format compliance, completeness (0-25 each); checks 6 required facts via regex; outputs summary.md with rankings and all_results.json
- tests/gambit_package_archive.py — End-to-end archive builder: generates writeup, structures sources/ with numbered directories, includes original files + relevance.txt per document
- tests/gambit_retrieval_e2e.py — Full pipeline test: 5 prompt variations, measures retrieval recall/precision (required vs noise docs), generation fact accuracy, end-to-end timing
- tests/results/form_fill_gambit/ — 120 individual model outputs + all_results.json + summary.md
- tests/results/retrieval_e2e_gambit/ — all_results.json, summary.md, IMPROVEMENT_JOURNEY.md

Enhance Tasks tab with guided generation workflow and form detection 
 

The Tasks tab has been enhanced with a guided generation workflow and form detection capabilities. This includes a new "Find Documents" step that uses hybrid BM25+kNN search, entity extraction, and abbreviation search to discover relevant documents. The workflow also includes a "Generate" step that produces content from selected documents only, and a "Refine" step that allows users to iterate on the output.

Implement guided Tasks workflow with form detection and export package

Add a guided document generation workflow under the "Tasks" tab that helps users generate accurate form content from indexed documents. The workflow includes prompt quality scoring, multi-phase document discovery, form-aware generation, and structured export packages.

The new Tasks workflow:
1. Prompt entry with live quality scoring via embedding search
2. Multi-phase document discovery: hybrid search → entity extraction → abbreviation search → prompt decomposition → Cohere Rerank
3. Document review with relevance snippets and checkbox selection
4. Form-aware generation: switches to plain-paragraph mode when application/form documents are detected
5. Iterative refinement with follow-up instructions
6. Export as PDF/DOCX/Markdown/ZIP package (includes source documents with relevance notes)
7. Task history persistence (localStorage) with configurable retention

Key backend changes:
- Added `/search/refine` endpoint for prompt decomposition + Cohere Rerank
- Added `/generate/export-package` endpoint for structured ZIP archives
- Updated `/tasks/generate` to support chunk-level retrieval, form detection, and post-processing
- Added query expansion to `/ask` endpoint for better retrieval
- Set default task model to NVIDIA Nemotron Super (100/100 gambit score)

Added three new test gambits:
- Form Fill Gambit: tests 120 combinations of models × document sets × prompt complexity
- Retrieval E2E Gambit: tests full pipeline from prompt to generation
- Package Archive Gambit: verifies structured export format

- backend/app/schemas.py — Added expanded_query field to AskResponse
- backend/app/search.py — Added search_chunks_grouped(), decompose_prompt(), refine_document_selection() functions
- backend/app/services.py — Added query expansion to run_ask()

Add Tasks tab with form-aware generation, refinement pipeline, and gambit benchmarks

This commit delivers the full Tasks tab workflow: a multi-phase document discovery pipeline (hybrid search → entity extraction → abbreviation search → prompt decomposition → Cohere Rerank → score cutoff → form auto-include), chunk-level context retrieval via search_chunks_grouped(), form-aware system prompt injection when application/form documents are detected, post-processing paragraph splitting via Nova Micro, task history persistence in localStorage, a workflow log widget visible in the UI, and a restructured /generate/export-package endpoint that produces a timestamped ZIP with per-document subdirectories and relevance.txt files. The default task model is changed from amazon.nova-pro-v1:0 to nvidia.nemotron-super-3-120b based on gambit benchmark results (100/100, 2.7s). The /ask pipeline gains Nova Micro query expansion plus dual-search merging, and AskResponse gains an expanded_query field. Gap-to-email prompts are de-personalized. Three gambit test scripts and 96 result files are added covering 120 model×docset×prompt combinations and end-to-end retrieval scoring.

Add Tasks workflow with guided generation, refinement, and benchmarking

Implemented a complete guided document generation workflow in the Tasks tab with live prompt quality scoring, multi-phase document discovery, chunk-level retrieval, form-aware generation, task history persistence, and structured ZIP export. Validated the pipeline with comprehensive gambit testing across 10 models, 4 document sets, and 3 prompt complexity levels (120 combinations), plus end-to-end retrieval testing. Selected NVIDIA Nemotron Super as the default task model based on 100/100 gambit score.

Add Task History, improve search, and add form detection for guided document generation

Added Task History feature to save and load previous task prompts and results. Users can now set how many previous tasks to keep (default: 5). Implemented a new search refinement endpoint that uses prompt decomposition and Cohere Rerank to improve document selection quality. Added form detection that automatically switches to plain-paragraph output when a form/application document is in the selected set. Updated the frontend to show workflow logs and task history. Modified the backend to use chunk-level retrieval for precision when users curate their document list. Changed the default task model to Nemotron Super based on gambit testing results. Added structured package export with source document relevance notes.

Add Tasks tab with guided document generation workflow

The Tasks tab introduces a guided workflow for generating document content from indexed documents. It includes a live prompt quality meter, hybrid search with entity extraction and abbreviation search, document review with relevance snippets, generation using only selected documents, refinement iteration, and export as PDF, DOCX, Markdown, or a structured ZIP package with source files and relevance summaries.

The implementation adds a new `/search/refine` endpoint that decomposes prompts into structured intent maps, performs entity matching, reranks using Cohere Rerank with content-focused queries, and applies score cutoffs to remove noise while preserving entity matches. The `/generate/export-package` endpoint now creates a structured archive with numbered source directories, original files, and relevance summaries explaining why each document was included.

The Tasks workflow also features form detection: when a form or application document is in the selected set, the system automatically switches to plain-paragraph output with no markdown headers or bullets, and post-processes wall-of-text into natural paragraphs using a topic-transition model.

Enhance banking statement processing with account metadata and transaction extraction

This commit adds comprehensive account-level metadata capture and advanced transaction extraction for banking documents. New database columns store account details (type, number, last4, holder name), while enhanced document processing extracts transactions from both structured JSON and raw OCR text using pattern matching. Banking service queries now support year and account filtering, and the UI displays transactions with detailed account information alongside cached Bedrock/Textract outputs.

Add document classification and upload history tracking

This commit introduces a document classification system to automatically categorize uploaded PDFs (banking, paycheck, utility, or unknown) and adds an upload history UI component to track all document processing activity. The classification logic uses configurable patterns to identify document types based on extracted text. The upload history page displays a chronological list of all uploaded documents with their classification results, processing timestamps, and file metadata. Additionally, AWS integration modules were enhanced with improved error handling and caching, and several service modules were updated to support the new classification workflow.

New:
- app/services/document_classifier.py — DocumentClassifier class with classify_document() method using regex patterns from config
- app/ui/upload_history.py — UploadHistory Streamlit component displaying document upload table with filters
- config/document_classification.yaml — classification patterns for banking (account_number, routing_number), paycheck (pay_period, net_pay, employer), and utility (account_number, due_date, service_address)
- PLAID_ROADMAP.md — roadmap document for Plaid API integration phases
- .cache/paycheck/bedrock/*.json (29 files) — cached Bedrock analysis results for paycheck PDFs from 2025-01 through 2026-02
- .cache/paycheck/textract/*.txt (29 files) — cached Textract OCR output for corresponding paycheck PDFs

Modified:
- .gitignore — added .cache/ directory exclusion
- app/integrations/aws_bedrock.py — added analyze_document_with_bedrock() function with caching and error handling
- app/integrations/aws_textract.py — added extract_text_from_pdf() function with S3 integration and caching
- app/services/banking_service.py — integrated DocumentClassifier for banking document validation
- app/services/document_service.py — added classify_and_route() method calling DocumentClassifier
- app/services/paycheck_service.py — integrated classification check before processing paycheck documents
- app/services/utility_ingestion_service.py — added document type validation using classifier
- app/ui/layout.py — added "Upload History" navigation link to sidebar
- documents/tasks.py — added classify_document_task() Celery task
- pages/2_Banking_Summary.py — integrated classification filter in document query
- pages/3_Utility_Summary.py — added classification metadata to utility document display
- pages/4_Paycheck_Summary.py — added classification confidence score display
- pages/6_Documents.py — added classification column to documents table
- pages/9_Settings.py — added document classification configuration section
- requirements.txt — added pyyaml>=6.0 for YAML config parsing

Add document classifier, upload history UI, and paycheck cache

This commit introduces a DocumentClassifier service backed by a new YAML configuration file, a reusable upload_history Streamlit component, and a PLAID_ROADMAP planning document. The paycheck processing pipeline is extended with 29 new Bedrock JSON extraction results and matching Textract raw-text files covering pay dates from 2025-01-07 through 2026-02-20. Supporting services, pages, and integrations are updated to wire in the new classifier and history UI.

New:
- .cache/paycheck/bedrock/ (29 files) — Bedrock extraction JSON results for Booz Allen Hamilton paychecks covering 2025-01-07 through 2026-02-20, each containing paycheck_details with earnings, taxes, pre/post-tax deductions, benefits, direct_deposit, accruals, and supplemental_earnings
- .cache/paycheck/textract/ (29 files) — Textract raw-text output files paired with each Bedrock JSON above
- PLAID_ROADMAP.md — new Plaid integration roadmap document
- app/services/document_classifier.py — new DocumentClassifier class backed by document_classification.yaml
- app/ui/upload_history.py — new Streamlit upload history component
- config/document_classification.yaml — new YAML config defining document classification rules

Add paycheck and utility document classification and upload history features

This commit introduces a document classification system for paychecks and utilities, along with an upload history feature. The key changes include:

- Added a new document classifier service with configuration for identifying paycheck and utility documents
- Created a UI component for displaying upload history
- Updated the document service to handle classification
- Enhanced the paycheck service to process classified documents
- Added a roadmap for Plaid integration
- Updated the banking, utility, and paycheck summary pages to work with the new classification system
- Modified the document page to show upload history
- Updated the settings page to include document classification options
- Added new requirements for the document classification functionality

The changes are designed to improve document organization and provide better visibility into uploaded documents.

Add document classification and caching infrastructure

Implemented a comprehensive document classification system with AWS integration for processing financial documents. Added caching mechanisms for both Textract OCR results and Bedrock AI analysis to improve performance and reduce API costs. The system now automatically classifies uploaded documents, extracts structured data, and maintains a persistent cache of processed results.

New:
- .cache/paycheck/bedrock/*.json — cached Bedrock AI analysis results for paycheck documents
- .cache/paycheck/textract/*.txt — cached Textract OCR extraction results for paycheck documents  
- PLAID_ROADMAP.md — development roadmap for Plaid integration features
